editorialSummary.text: Down-to-earth restaurant specializing in BBQ meats & hearty burgers, plus craft beers on tap.

text.text: Worth the wait and worth the price. I would always drive by and notice the line.I especially wanted to try this location. Everything was good.I've tried the mexican dark lager. It was great, it was very light for being kind of dark craft beer. A lemonade and a michelada, were also good very refreshing. And now comes the food. I got the El trio and this did not disappoint the pork ribs. The sausages and the brisket all good lean, flavorful, and you could literally cut these all with a plastic spoon. The ribs were my favorite. I could not stop eating them. And the science were the best. Kimchi fried rice mac and cheese red potato salad.All good and full of flavor. The mac and cheese crunchy cheesy fresh was so good.The kimchi fried rice with fried pork belly in.It was my personal favorite.I ate the whole thing. Yes, it was a very long wine. Yes, you did have to wait and yes, it was very pricey, but this was one of the best barbecue that I had, since i've been back in california, we're definitely stopping by here again.

text.text:
Moo’s Craft Barbeque absolutely lives up to the hype of being some of the best Texas BBQ in Los Angeles. But fair warning: come prepared to wait… and wait… and wait. There’s usually a line before they even open, and it does not move quickly. People take their time ordering, and the food itself takes a bit to come out — but honestly, it’s worth it.

The bar is separate from the main ordering line, but they’ve got a great drink menu.

The must-order item is the Korean sticky burnt ends. The glaze is absolutely unreal and easily one of the best bites of BBQ I’ve had in LA. Also, the mac & cheese is ridiculously creamy and comforting. Pro tip: even though it’s not on the menu, they do have sweet tea if you ask.

The music was 👌🏼

If you’re serious about BBQ, this spot is a pilgrimage. 5/5.

text.text:
for a Michelin, Bib Gourmand restaurant, I really did expect a lot. food influencers hyped the place up a lot as well.

first off, i told the cashier I wanted my order for takeout, which he understood because he told me not to sit at any of the tables to preserve space for the dine-in guests, but then when it came time to pick up my food, it was on trays for dine-in, which was also when i realized it said dine-in on my receipt. so I had to tell the staff member that it was to-go and not dine-in, which she seemed visibly irritated about (understandable) and quickly packaged the food.

as for the food, the meats (brisket, ribs, and sausage) were way less juicy than I expected. yes it was tender, but a little dry. I felt like i had to dip it in the barbeque sauce for it to taste great. the sides were not bad, coleslaw and cucumber were standard, but the mac n' cheese could use a little extra seasoning.

overall, i think Moo's Craft Barbecue is decent bbq if ur in the area, but it's not worth the trip if you're coming from far away. like another reviewer said, I honestly had better food at Lucille's.

text.text:
Got here at 1030am on a Friday. First in line. About 11am there was perhaps 10 of us in line.

Ordered food; 1lb brisket, 1lb ribs 1/2lb, pork belly, one jalapeno cheddar sausage, large salad, and small kimchi fried rice. $120 out the door.

I have followed this place for sometime. Perhaps around 2020/2021, and finally got to try it. Ambience, great. I can smell the bbq a block away. Very clean.

Food was incredible. Brisket is probably the 2nd best I've had under Franklins in Austin TX. Definitely the best I've had in California. Very tender. Very moist. Fat was very well rendered. Flavor was great. Not nearly as salty as my last franklins experience, which I think my girlfriend preferred.

Sausage had a great snap. Has more than enough spice to it.

The really winner was the Korean Glazed Pork Belly. Extremely tender and just one of the best bites of meat I've ever had.

Both the fried rice and the cucumber salad were great sides. Certainly the best sides we've ever had at a BBQ restaurant.

text.text: Went for my B Day dinner. It was FANTASTIC. I went all out and I am glad I did. Got the Pork Ribs, The Poblano Sausage, The Thick Burger, side of fries, Kimchi fried rice, and a large Mac and Cheese. I  also got the Banana pudding for dessert. I went full big back mode. Don’t judge me. LOL.  Everything was delish and the Brisket burger devoured first. All the sides were great epically the fries and Mac. Also the staff was very kind and the girl taking my order gave me the Pecan Bar for my B day. (Not pictured) I am eating it now as I write this review a day later and it is great!!! The guy behind the bar was rocking a cool retro PoKemon Shirt. Shout to all my old school gamers and I got a can cocktail to go with the food. The restaurant has a great look and an outdoor seating area. I lived in GA for over 10 years so I know my barbecue. When you can drop in and grab a bite!!!! Also all that food cost me $100 before tip. Just FYI.
